response.justification: The candidate is fundamentally misaligned with this role despite strong general software engineering credentials. Critical disqualifying gaps include: native Japanese language proficiency (a stated requirement), zero background in security technologies and networking protocols (the core domain), minimal C/UNIX systems programming (only ~6 months at National Instruments), and no Linux kernel modification experience. The career trajectory over the past decade has diverged sharply toward web/app development, moving away from embedded systems work needed for this mid-senior embedded security role.
strengths: 18+ years of demonstrated software craftsmanship with senior/staff-level positions and proven ability to architect testable, high-quality systems
Experience with embedded systems, robotics, and infrastructure-level work (DevOps, CI/CD, system administration), showing some exposure to low-level concerns
Multi-language proficiency and proven capacity to learn diverse technical domains quickly across platforms
concerns: Missing native Japanese language proficiency—a stated requirement that eliminates ability to communicate with stakeholders and access primary technical resources
No security domain expertise: lacks background in encryption, authentication protocols (802.1X, MACsec), attack detection, and tamper detection—core responsibilities of this role

Severely limited C/embedded firmware background with only 6 months of relevant C development (2014-2015); recent 10-year career focused on JavaScript/TypeScript, web/app development, and away from systems programming

key_factors.factor: Security and networking domain expertise gap
